content.content:
- content: 'The HR Service Center answers general employee-process questions and

    maintains case correspondence for follow-up. Employees may attach

    supporting documents to an HR case so that service representatives have

    context for the question being discussed. A case can remain open while a

    representative responds, requests clarification, or records follow-up.


    Managers may also be contacted for business review or approval questions

    when a process requires their involvement. The state of an HR support case

    describes the support conversation; process-specific transactions follow

    the workflow of the relevant business process.

    '

args.query: Harvard University travel reimbursement non travel expense reimbursement system receipt

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Travel & Reimbursement | Financial Administration

  - url: https://finance.harvard.edu/job-aid-topic/travel-and-reimbursements

  - source: Harvard University Financial Administration

  - domain: finance.harvard.edu


  ## Content


  TRAVEL & REIMBURSEMENT


  Harvard Financial Administration maintains a collection of University resources
  for travel, business expenses, reimbursements, and payment-card activity.


  Business Expense Reimbursements Policy


  This policy establishes when Harvard will pay for non-travel business expenses incurred
  by individuals conducting business on the University''s behalf.


  Travel Policy


  This policy establishes how Harvard University reimburses individuals for travel
  expenses incurred while conducting University business. It includes appendices covering
  transportation, lodging, meals, entertainment, and combined personal and business
  travel.


  Employee Reimbursement / Corporate Card Payment


  Corporate Card payments and employee reimbursements are made using Concur, Harvard''s
  Travel & Expense Reimbursement System.


  Non-Employee Reimbursement


  Students and non-employees requesting reimbursement for expenses incurred while
  completing official Harvard business use the Buy-to-Pay Non-Employee Reimbursement
  Form. This is a separate resource from the employee reimbursement route.


  Missing Receipt


  Harvard provides a Missing Receipt Affidavit resource when a reimbursement is requested
  and the original receipt is unavailable.


  Other Resources


  The page also links to foreign and domestic per diem rates, University record-retention
  guidance, the Buy-to-Pay procurement system, reimbursement guidance for foreign
  nationals, related policies and procedures, forms and templates, job aids, system
  applications, training resources, and Financial Administration contact information.


  The employee reimbursement entry identifies Concur as Harvard''s Travel & Expense
  Reimbursement System. The non-employee form is separately identified for students
  and non-employees, rather than as the employee reimbursement route.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Travel & Reimbursement | Financial Administration
url: https://finance.harvard.edu/job-aid-topic/travel-and-reimbursements

Your receipt is attached to the HR case, but it **has not been submitted as a formal expense report**. An HR case attachment provides context for the case; it does not initiate the reimbursement workflow.

For Harvard employee reimbursement, you should:

1. Sign in to **Concur**, Harvard’s Travel & Expense Reimbursement System.
2. Create a new **expense report/claim**.
3. Enter the expense details and attach the receipt there.
4. Complete any required business purpose, cost object/accounting, and approval information.
5. Submit the report in Concur and monitor it for approval or requests for clarification.

Harvard’s Financial Administration page confirms that employee reimbursements are processed through Concur:  
[Travel & Reimbursement — Harvard Financial Administration](https://finance.harvard.edu/job-aid-topic/travel-and-reimbursements)

You can leave the receipt in the HR case for reference, but don’t rely on the HR case alone for reimbursement.

final_answer:
Your receipt is attached to the HR case, but it **has not been submitted as a formal expense report**. An HR case attachment provides context for the case; it does not initiate the reimbursement workflow.

For Harvard employee reimbursement, you should:

1. Sign in to **Concur**, Harvard’s Travel & Expense Reimbursement System.
2. Create a new **expense report/claim**.
3. Enter the expense details and attach the receipt there.
4. Complete any required business purpose, cost object/accounting, and approval information.
5. Submit the report in Concur and monitor it for approval or requests for clarification.

Harvard’s Financial Administration page confirms that employee reimbursements are processed through Concur:  
[Travel & Reimbursement — Harvard Financial Administration](https://finance.harvard.edu/job-aid-topic/travel-and-reimbursements)

You can leave the receipt in the HR case for reference, but don’t rely on the HR case alone for reimbursement.

        [^.!?\n]{0,100}\b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b )
(?! .* \b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b
        [^.!?\n]{0,120}\b(?:or|alternatively|depending\s+on|whichever)\b
        [^.!?\n]{0,100}
        \b(?:Workday|Expensify|ERP|another|other|your\s+(?:company|organization)|
             company(?:'s)?|organization(?:'s)?)\b )
(?! .* \b(?:Workday|Expensify|ERP|another|other)\b
        [^.!?\n]{0,120}\b(?:or|alternatively)\b
        [^.!?\n]{0,100}\b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b )
(?=
  .* (?:
    (?:use|submit|file|route|send|process|processed|handle|handled|manage|managed|made|
       complete|create|start|open|go\s+through|proceed\s+through|must\s+use|
       should\s+use|need(?:s)?\s+to\s+use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}\b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b
    |
    \b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:official|formal|required|designated|employee\s+reimbursement|
       expense\s+report|reimbursement\s+system|workflow|portal|system|
       submit|file|use)
  )
)
(?=.*\b(?:expense\s+(?:report|claim)|
           reimbursement\s+(?:claim|request|process|workflow)|
           employee\s+reimbursement)\b)
(?=
  .* (?:
    (?:\bHR\b|human\s+resources|HR\s+(?:case|ticket)|
       support\s+(?:case|ticket)|case\s+attachment)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      does\s+not|doesn't|is\s+not|isn't|cannot|can't|will\s+not|won't|
      only|just|support[-\s]*only|not\s+(?:enough|sufficient)|
      does\s+not\s+(?:replace|submit|trigger|constitute|count)|
      doesn't\s+(?:replace|submit|trigger|constitute|count)|
      rather\s+than|instead\s+of
    )
    |
    (?:
      does\s+not|doesn't|is\s+not|isn't|cannot|can't|will\s+not|won't|
      only|just|support[-\s]*only|not\s+(?:enough|sufficient)|
      does\s+not\s+(?:replace|submit|trigger|constitute|count)|
      doesn't\s+(?:replace|submit|trigger|constitute|count)|
      rather\s+than|instead\s+of
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:\bHR\b|human\s+resources|HR\s+(?:case|ticket)|
       support\s+(?:case|ticket)|case\s+attachment)
    |
    (?:\bHR\b|human\s+resources|HR\s+(?:case|ticket)|
       support\s+(?:case|ticket)|case\s+attachment)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}(?:but|however|instead|rather)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}\b(?:SAP\s+)?Concur\b

)
.*\Z
